{"key_event":"Devolution, rights, and post-9/11 security expansion","reasoning":"Civil society remained extremely strong and gained modestly from devolution, the Human Rights Act, FOI, courts, NGOs, and mass protest, though counterterror laws, surveillance, and executive dominance limited the increase. State power also rose modestly as New Labour combined strong central management, expanded security powers, restored Northern Ireland institutions, and massive bank rescues, despite devolution and financial-regulatory weakness checking Westminster’s unconstrained reach.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":9.5,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.3}
